title: × Agrohordeum langei
intro:
There is no information available on the plant × Agrohordeum langei. However, Agropyron cristatum is a synonym for Elymus lanceolatus, and Hordeum vulgare is the common barley plant but I found Agropyron langei  is a synonym of Elymus lanceolatus. 

Here's a paragraph about Elymus lanceolatus:

Elymus lanceolatus, also known as lance-leaved wildrye, is a type of grass native to North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Elymus lanceolatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Elymus lanceolatus is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
taxonomy: × Agrohordeum langei is a hybrid of the genus Agropyron and Hordeum, two genera of grasses in the family Poaceae. It is a hybrid species created through plant breeding or natural hybridization. As a hybrid, its classification may not follow the traditional binomial nomenclature used for non-hybrid species. The classification of × Agrohordeum langei may be considered as a nothospecies, a hybrid species that is treated as a distinct taxon. The classification of × Agrohordeum langei may also be considered as a hybrid of Hordeum and Agropyron, but it is not clear which specific species within these genera it is a hybrid of.
parts: I couldn't find any information on the medicinal parts of the plant × Agrohordeum langei.
